tagline: Painter of folk and mandala art. Seeker of stillness. Believer that the right piece of art can change the energy of a room — and the person in it.
story_p1: I didn't set out to paint. I found my way to it during a period of stillness — a time when I needed something to hold my attention and quiet my mind. The first mandala I drew was small, imperfect, and entirely mine. Folk art came later, through a deep curiosity about the stories and symbols passed down through generations. That's when I understood what painting really is: a practice in patience, memory, and surrender.
story_p2: What started as a personal ritual became something I couldn't stop sharing. Friends and family began noticing the paintings on my walls. Then came requests. Then commissions. And slowly — a studio practice.
story_p3: Today, every painting I make carries that same original intention: to create something that gives the viewer a moment of genuine stillness in a world that rarely slows down.
quote: A painting is never truly finished on its own — it completes itself in the space it inhabits, and in the eye of the person who meets it.
